text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: migraine
Description: A migraine is characterized by severe, throbbing pain, often on one side of the head, and can be accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light and sound.
Advice: Take pain relief medication like paracetamol or ibuprofen and rest in a quiet, dark room.
When to See Doctor: If you have a bad throbbing pain at the front or side of your head, or if painkillers do not help and your headache gets worse.

metadata.advice: Take pain relief medication like paracetamol or ibuprofen and rest in a quiet, dark room.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have a bad throbbing pain at the front or side of your head, or if painkillers do not help and your headache gets worse.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: cluster headache
Description: Cluster headaches are extremely painful headaches that occur in cyclical patterns or clusters, often waking individuals from sleep.
Advice: Seek medical advice for appropriate treatment options, as these headaches can be very severe.
When to See Doctor: If you experience severe headaches that occur in clusters or have a bad throbbing pain.

metadata.advice: Seek medical advice for appropriate treatment options, as these headaches can be very severe.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe headaches that occur in clusters or have a bad throbbing pain.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: tension headache
Description: Tension headaches are often caused by stress, anxiety, or poor posture, leading to a dull, aching sensation around the head.
Advice: Practice relaxation techniques, maintain good posture, and consider over-the-counter pain relief.
When to See Doctor: If your headache keeps coming back or if it is accompanied by other concerning symptoms.

metadata.advice: Practice relaxation techniques, maintain good posture, and consider over-the-counter pain relief.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If your headache keeps coming back or if it is accompanied by other concerning symptoms.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: hormonal headache
Description: Hormonal headaches can occur due to fluctuations in hormones, often related to menstrual cycles or menopause.
Advice: Keep track of your headaches and discuss with your doctor about potential treatments or lifestyle changes.
When to See Doctor: If you regularly get headaches before or during your period or if they worsen significantly.

metadata.advice: Keep track of your headaches and discuss with your doctor about potential treatments or lifestyle changes.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you regularly get headaches before or during your period or if they worsen significantly.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: dehydration headache
Description: Dehydration headaches occur when the body loses more fluids than it takes in, leading to pain and discomfort.
Advice: Drink plenty of water and avoid alcohol to help alleviate the headache.
When to See Doctor: If headaches persist despite hydration or if you experience symptoms of severe dehydration.

metadata.advice: Drink plenty of water and avoid alcohol to help alleviate the headache.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If headaches persist despite hydration or if you experience symptoms of severe dehydration.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: common cold
Description: A cough is often associated with a common cold, which is caused by viral infections and typically resolves on its own within a few weeks.
Advice: Rest, drink plenty of fluids, and consider using paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain relief.
When to See Doctor: See a GP if you've had a cough for more than 3 weeks or if you feel very unwell.

metadata.advice: Rest, drink plenty of fluids, and consider using paracetamol or ibuprofen for pain relief.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: See a GP if you've had a cough for more than 3 weeks or if you feel very unwell.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: bronchitis
Description: Bronchitis can cause a persistent cough due to inflammation of the bronchial tubes, often following a cold or flu.
Advice: Stay hydrated and rest; over-the-counter cough medicines may help alleviate symptoms.
When to See Doctor: Seek urgent advice if your cough is very bad, you have chest pain, or you're coughing up blood.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and rest; over-the-counter cough medicines may help alleviate symptoms.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: Seek urgent advice if your cough is very bad, you have chest pain, or you're coughing up blood.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: COVID-19
Description: A cough can be a symptom of COVID-19, often accompanied by other symptoms such as fever and fatigue.
Advice: Isolate yourself and consider getting tested for COVID-19; rest and drink fluids.
When to See Doctor: Contact a doctor if you have difficulty breathing or your cough worsens significantly.

metadata.advice: Isolate yourself and consider getting tested for COVID-19; rest and drink fluids.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: Contact a doctor if you have difficulty breathing or your cough worsens significantly.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: allergies
Description: Coughing can result from allergies, such as hay fever, where irritants trigger a cough reflex.
Advice: Avoid allergens and consider antihistamines; staying hydrated can also help.
When to See Doctor: Consult a GP if your symptoms persist or worsen despite avoiding allergens.

metadata.advice: Avoid allergens and consider antihistamines; staying hydrated can also help.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: Consult a GP if your symptoms persist or worsen despite avoiding allergens.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: acid reflux
Description: Coughing may occur due to acid reflux, where stomach acid irritates the throat and triggers a cough.
Advice: Avoid large meals and lying down after eating; consider over-the-counter antacids.
When to See Doctor: See a doctor if you experience persistent cough along with chest pain or difficulty swallowing.

metadata.advice: Avoid large meals and lying down after eating; consider over-the-counter antacids.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: See a doctor if you experience persistent cough along with chest pain or difficulty swallowing.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: cold
Description: A common viral infection that affects the upper respiratory tract, often accompanied by a high temperature as the body fights the virus.
Advice: Get plenty of rest and stay hydrated. Consider taking paracetamol or ibuprofen if uncomfortable.
When to See Doctor: If the fever persists or worsens despite home treatment.

metadata.advice: Get plenty of rest and stay hydrated. Consider taking paracetamol or ibuprofen if uncomfortable.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the fever persists or worsens despite home treatment.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: flu
Description: An infectious disease caused by influenza viruses, characterized by fever, chills, and body aches as the immune system responds to the infection.
Advice: Rest and drink fluids. Use paracetamol or ibuprofen for discomfort.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms do not improve or worsen after home treatment.

metadata.advice: Rest and drink fluids. Use paracetamol or ibuprofen for discomfort.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms do not improve or worsen after home treatment.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: infection
Description: A high temperature can indicate the body is fighting an infection, which may be bacterial or viral.
Advice: Stay hydrated and rest. Over-the-counter medications can help alleviate discomfort.
When to See Doctor: If the fever is very high or does not subside with treatment.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and rest. Over-the-counter medications can help alleviate discomfort.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the fever is very high or does not subside with treatment.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: dehydration
Description: Fever can lead to dehydration, especially if accompanied by sweating or other symptoms that cause fluid loss.
Advice: Drink plenty of fluids to stay hydrated, aiming for light yellow urine.
When to See Doctor: If you experience severe thirst, dry mouth, or reduced urination.

metadata.advice: Drink plenty of fluids to stay hydrated, aiming for light yellow urine.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe thirst, dry mouth, or reduced urination.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: serious infection
Description: In some cases, a persistent high temperature may indicate a more serious underlying infection that requires medical evaluation.
Advice: Seek medical attention if the fever is very high or does not improve with home care.
When to See Doctor: If the fever is accompanied by severe symptoms or does not respond to treatment.

metadata.advice: Seek medical attention if the fever is very high or does not improve with home care.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the fever is accompanied by severe symptoms or does not respond to treatment.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Sleep apnoea
Description: A condition where breathing repeatedly stops and starts during sleep, leading to excessive daytime fatigue.
Advice: Consult a GP for a sleep study if you experience loud snoring or gasping during sleep.
When to See Doctor: If you notice loud snoring or gasping noises during sleep, or if fatigue persists despite adequate sleep.

metadata.advice: Consult a GP for a sleep study if you experience loud snoring or gasping during sleep.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you notice loud snoring or gasping noises during sleep, or if fatigue persists despite adequate sleep.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Iron deficiency anaemia
Description: A condition characterized by a lack of healthy red blood cells due to insufficient iron, causing fatigue and weakness.
Advice: Consider dietary changes to include more iron-rich foods and consult a GP for blood tests.
When to See Doctor: If you experience fatigue along with shortness of breath, heart palpitations, or pale skin.

metadata.advice: Consider dietary changes to include more iron-rich foods and consult a GP for blood tests.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience fatigue along with shortness of breath, heart palpitations, or pale skin.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Diabetes
Description: A chronic condition that affects how your body processes blood sugar, leading to extreme fatigue among other symptoms.
Advice: Monitor your blood sugar levels and consult a GP if you experience increased thirst and frequent urination.
When to See Doctor: If fatigue is accompanied by excessive thirst, frequent urination, or unexplained weight loss.

metadata.advice: Monitor your blood sugar levels and consult a GP if you experience increased thirst and frequent urination.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue is accompanied by excessive thirst, frequent urination, or unexplained weight loss.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Overactive thyroid (hyperthyroidism)
Description: A condition where the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like fatigue and anxiety.
Advice: Consult a GP for a thyroid function test if you experience nervousness or muscle weakness.
When to See Doctor: If you feel excessively tired and have symptoms like anxiety, irritability, or muscle weakness.

metadata.advice: Consult a GP for a thyroid function test if you experience nervousness or muscle weakness.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you feel excessively tired and have symptoms like anxiety, irritability, or muscle weakness.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Myalgic encephalomyelitis or chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS)
Description: A complex disorder characterized by extreme fatigue that lasts for at least six months and is not improved by rest.
Advice: Seek medical advice for a proper diagnosis and management plan if fatigue severely impacts daily activities.
When to See Doctor: If fatigue persists for more than three months and is accompanied by cognitive difficulties or flu-like symptoms.

metadata.possible_condition: Myalgic encephalomyelitis or chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS)
metadata.advice: Seek medical advice for a proper diagnosis and management plan if fatigue severely impacts daily activities.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue persists for more than three months and is accompanied by cognitive difficulties or flu-like symptoms.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: Norovirus
Description: Norovirus is a highly contagious virus that causes gastroenteritis, leading to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
Advice: Stay hydrated and rest; consider seeing a GP if symptoms persist.
When to See Doctor: If you are feeling sick and do not feel better in a few days or if you experience severe dehydration.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and rest; consider seeing a GP if symptoms persist.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you are feeling sick and do not feel better in a few days or if you experience severe dehydration.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: Acid reflux
Description: Acid reflux occurs when stomach acid flows back into the esophagus, causing discomfort and nausea, especially after eating.
Advice: Avoid trigger foods and consider eating smaller meals more frequently.
When to See Doctor: If you have persistent nausea along with heartburn or bloating after eating.

metadata.advice: Avoid trigger foods and consider eating smaller meals more frequently.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have persistent nausea along with heartburn or bloating after eating.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: Migraine
Description: Migraines can cause severe headaches accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light or sound.
Advice: Rest in a quiet, dark room and stay hydrated; consult a GP for appropriate treatment.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is frequent and accompanied by severe headaches.

metadata.advice: Rest in a quiet, dark room and stay hydrated; consult a GP for appropriate treatment.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is frequent and accompanied by severe headaches.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: Morning sickness
Description: Morning sickness is common during pregnancy and is characterized by nausea and vomiting, especially in the first trimester.
Advice: Eat small, frequent meals and stay hydrated; consult a healthcare provider if symptoms are severe.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is severe and affects your ability to keep food or fluids down.

metadata.advice: Eat small, frequent meals and stay hydrated; consult a healthcare provider if symptoms are severe.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is severe and affects your ability to keep food or fluids down.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: Motion sickness
Description: Motion sickness occurs when there is a conflict between the sensory signals received by the brain, leading to nausea during travel.
Advice: Try to focus on the horizon and avoid reading while in motion; consider over-the-counter remedies.
When to See Doctor: If nausea occurs frequently during travel and affects your ability to travel comfortably.

metadata.advice: Try to focus on the horizon and avoid reading while in motion; consider over-the-counter remedies.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea occurs frequently during travel and affects your ability to travel comfortably.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Trapped wind (flatulence)
Description: Trapped wind causes bloating and discomfort in the stomach, often leading to stomach pain.
Advice: Try to move around, as physical activity can help release trapped gas.
When to See Doctor: If bloating does not go away or keeps coming back.

metadata.advice: Try to move around, as physical activity can help release trapped gas.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If bloating does not go away or keeps coming back.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Indigestion
Description: Indigestion can cause feelings of fullness, bloating, and stomach pain after eating.
Advice: Avoid large meals and spicy foods; consider over-the-counter antacids.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms persist or worsen despite home treatment.

metadata.advice: Avoid large meals and spicy foods; consider over-the-counter antacids.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist or worsen despite home treatment.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Constipation
Description: Constipation can lead to abdominal discomfort and pain due to difficulty in bowel movements.
Advice: Increase fiber intake and stay hydrated; consider a mild laxative if needed.
When to See Doctor: If you cannot poo or have severe pain.

metadata.advice: Increase fiber intake and stay hydrated; consider a mild laxative if needed.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you cannot poo or have severe pain.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Stomach bug or food poisoning
Description: A stomach bug or food poisoning can cause watery poo, nausea, and vomiting along with stomach pain.
Advice: Stay hydrated and rest; seek medical advice if symptoms are severe.
When to See Doctor: If diarrhea does not go away after a few days or if you are vomiting blood.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and rest; seek medical advice if symptoms are severe.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If diarrhea does not go away after a few days or if you are vomiting blood.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Appendicitis
Description: Appendicitis presents as sudden pain in the lower right-hand side of the abdomen, often severe.
Advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect appendicitis.
When to See Doctor: If you have sudden severe pain or it hurts when you touch your stomach.

metadata.advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect appendicitis.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have sudden severe pain or it hurts when you touch your stomach.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: viral infection
Description: A common cause of sore throat, often accompanied by other symptoms like a runny nose and cough.
Advice: Drink plenty of fluids and rest; consider over-the-counter pain relief if needed.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or worsen significantly.

metadata.advice: Drink plenty of fluids and rest; consider over-the-counter pain relief if needed.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or worsen significantly.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: bacterial infection (e.g., strep throat)
Description: A bacterial infection that can cause severe sore throat, often with fever and swollen lymph nodes.
Advice: Consult a GP for a possible antibiotic prescription if you suspect a bacterial infection.
When to See Doctor: If you have a high fever, difficulty swallowing, or symptoms that do not improve.

metadata.advice: Consult a GP for a possible antibiotic prescription if you suspect a bacterial infection.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have a high fever, difficulty swallowing, or symptoms that do not improve.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: allergies
Description: Allergies can cause throat irritation and soreness due to post-nasal drip.
Advice: Avoid allergens and consider antihistamines if necessary.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms are severe or persistent despite avoiding allergens.

metadata.advice: Avoid allergens and consider antihistamines if necessary.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms are severe or persistent despite avoiding allergens.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD)
Description: Acid reflux can irritate the throat, leading to soreness and discomfort.
Advice: Avoid spicy foods and eat smaller meals; consider over-the-counter antacids.
When to See Doctor: If you experience frequent heartburn or if symptoms worsen.

metadata.advice: Avoid spicy foods and eat smaller meals; consider over-the-counter antacids.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience frequent heartburn or if symptoms worsen.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: throat cancer
Description: Though rare, persistent sore throat can be a sign of throat cancer, especially if accompanied by other alarming symptoms.
Advice: Seek medical evaluation if you have a sore throat that lasts longer than three weeks.
When to See Doctor: If you notice lumps in your neck, difficulty swallowing, or unexplained weight loss.

metadata.advice: Seek medical evaluation if you have a sore throat that lasts longer than three weeks.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you notice lumps in your neck, difficulty swallowing, or unexplained weight loss.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: vertigo
Description: Vertigo is a sensation of spinning or dizziness, often related to inner ear problems.
Advice: Lie down until the dizziness passes, then get up slowly.
When to See Doctor: If you're finding it harder to hear or speak, or if you have double vision.

metadata.advice: Lie down until the dizziness passes, then get up slowly.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you're finding it harder to hear or speak, or if you have double vision.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: dehydration
Description: Dehydration can cause dizziness due to a lack of fluids in the body.
Advice: Drink plenty of fluids, especially water.
When to See Doctor: If dizziness does not go away or keeps coming back.

metadata.advice: Drink plenty of fluids, especially water.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness does not go away or keeps coming back.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: anxiety
Description: Anxiety can lead to feelings of dizziness or lightheadedness.
Advice: Move slowly and carefully to avoid worsening the dizziness.
When to See Doctor: If you have other symptoms like changes to your pulse or fainting.

metadata.advice: Move slowly and carefully to avoid worsening the dizziness.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have other symptoms like changes to your pulse or fainting.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: labyrinthitis
Description: Labyrinthitis is an inner ear infection that can cause dizziness and balance issues.
Advice: Get plenty of rest and avoid sudden movements.
When to See Doctor: If you experience ringing in your ears or other auditory changes.

metadata.advice: Get plenty of rest and avoid sudden movements.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience ringing in your ears or other auditory changes.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: postural hypotension
Description: Postural hypotension occurs when standing up suddenly causes a drop in blood pressure, leading to dizziness.
Advice: Do not get up suddenly after sitting or lying down.
When to See Doctor: If you experience numbness or weakness in your face, arms, or legs.

metadata.advice: Do not get up suddenly after sitting or lying down.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience numbness or weakness in your face, arms, or legs.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: Tension Headaches
Description: Tension headaches are the most common type of headache among adults and teens, causing mild to moderate pain that can come and go over time.
Advice: Practice relaxation techniques and consider over-the-counter pain relievers.
When to See Doctor: If headaches become more frequent or severe, or if they disrupt daily activities.

metadata.advice: Practice relaxation techniques and consider over-the-counter pain relievers.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If headaches become more frequent or severe, or if they disrupt daily activities.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: Migraine Headaches
Description: Migraine headaches are characterized by pounding, throbbing pain and can last from 4 hours to 3 days, often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light.
Advice: Identify and avoid triggers, and consult a doctor for possible prescription medications.
When to See Doctor: If migraines occur more than once a month or if the severity increases.

metadata.advice: Identify and avoid triggers, and consult a doctor for possible prescription medications.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If migraines occur more than once a month or if the severity increases.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: Cluster Headaches
Description: Cluster headaches are extremely severe and can cause intense burning or piercing pain around one eye, often accompanied by eye redness and nasal congestion.
Advice: Seek immediate medical attention for severe pain and consider preventive treatments.
When to See Doctor: If you experience sudden, severe headaches or changes in headache patterns.

metadata.advice: Seek immediate medical attention for severe pain and consider preventive treatments.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience sudden, severe headaches or changes in headache patterns.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: Sinus Headaches
Description: Sinus headaches occur when the sinuses become inflamed, leading to pain and pressure in the forehead, cheeks, and around the eyes.
Advice: Use decongestants and pain relievers, and stay hydrated.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or worsen over time.

metadata.advice: Use decongestants and pain relievers, and stay hydrated.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or worsen over time.

text:
Symptom: headache
Possible Condition: Rebound Headaches
Description: Rebound headaches occur from overuse of pain medications, leading to a cycle of frequent headaches.
Advice: Limit the use of pain medications and consult a healthcare provider for a treatment plan.
When to See Doctor: If headaches persist despite medication use or if they worsen.

metadata.advice: Limit the use of pain medications and consult a healthcare provider for a treatment plan.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If headaches persist despite medication use or if they worsen.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: COVID-19
Description: COVID-19 is an infectious disease caused by the coronavirus, and a common symptom is a fever, which indicates the body's response to the infection.
Advice: Monitor your symptoms and consider getting tested for COVID-19 if you have a fever.
When to See Doctor: If your fever is persistent or accompanied by difficulty breathing, chest pain, or confusion.

metadata.advice: Monitor your symptoms and consider getting tested for COVID-19 if you have a fever.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If your fever is persistent or accompanied by difficulty breathing, chest pain, or confusion.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: Influenza
Description: Influenza, or the flu, is a viral infection that can cause a high fever as part of the body's immune response.
Advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and consider antiviral medications if symptoms are severe.
When to See Doctor: If your fever exceeds 104 F (40 C) or if you experience severe symptoms.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and consider antiviral medications if symptoms are severe.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If your fever exceeds 104 F (40 C) or if you experience severe symptoms.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: Bacterial Infection
Description: Bacterial infections, such as strep throat or pneumonia, often present with fever as the body fights off the infection.
Advice: Seek medical attention for appropriate antibiotics if a bacterial infection is suspected.
When to See Doctor: If fever is accompanied by severe pain, rash, or persistent vomiting.

metadata.advice: Seek medical attention for appropriate antibiotics if a bacterial infection is suspected.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If fever is accompanied by severe pain, rash, or persistent vomiting.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: Heat Exhaustion
Description: Heat exhaustion occurs when the body overheats, leading to symptoms including fever, especially in hot weather.
Advice: Move to a cooler place, hydrate, and rest to recover from heat exhaustion.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms worsen or do not improve with self-care measures.

metadata.advice: Move to a cooler place, hydrate, and rest to recover from heat exhaustion.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms worsen or do not improve with self-care measures.

text:
Symptom: fever
Possible Condition: Viral Infection
Description: Various viral infections can cause fever as the immune system reacts to the virus.
Advice: Rest and take over-the-counter medications to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
When to See Doctor: If the fever lasts more than three days or is very high.

metadata.advice: Rest and take over-the-counter medications to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the fever lasts more than three days or is very high.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: Common Cold
Description: A common cold can cause a cough as a symptom, often accompanied by other symptoms like a runny nose and sore throat.
Advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and consider over-the-counter medications to relieve symptoms.
When to See Doctor: If the cough persists for more than 10 days or is accompanied by a high fever.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated, rest, and consider over-the-counter medications to relieve symptoms.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the cough persists for more than 10 days or is accompanied by a high fever.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: Flu (Influenza)
Description: The flu can lead to a severe cough, often with body aches and fever, indicating a viral infection.
Advice: Rest, drink fluids, and consult a healthcare provider if symptoms worsen.
When to See Doctor: If you experience difficulty breathing or chest pain.

metadata.advice: Rest, drink fluids, and consult a healthcare provider if symptoms worsen.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience difficulty breathing or chest pain.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: Chronic Bronchitis
Description: Chronic bronchitis is characterized by a persistent cough that produces mucus, often due to long-term irritation of the airways.
Advice: Avoid irritants like smoke and consider seeking treatment for underlying conditions.
When to See Doctor: If the cough lasts longer than eight weeks or worsens over time.

metadata.advice: Avoid irritants like smoke and consider seeking treatment for underlying conditions.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the cough lasts longer than eight weeks or worsens over time.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: Allergies
Description: Allergies can trigger a cough due to postnasal drip or irritation in the throat.
Advice: Identify and avoid allergens, and consider antihistamines for relief.
When to See Doctor: If the cough is persistent and accompanied by other allergy symptoms.

metadata.advice: Identify and avoid allergens, and consider antihistamines for relief.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the cough is persistent and accompanied by other allergy symptoms.

text:
Symptom: cough
Possible Condition: Asthma
Description: Asthma can cause a chronic cough, often accompanied by wheezing and shortness of breath due to airway inflammation.
Advice: Use prescribed inhalers and avoid known triggers.
When to See Doctor: If the cough worsens or if you experience severe difficulty breathing.

metadata.advice: Use prescribed inhalers and avoid known triggers.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If the cough worsens or if you experience severe difficulty breathing.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Anemia
Description: Anemia is a condition where you lack enough healthy red blood cells to carry adequate oxygen to your body's tissues, leading to feelings of fatigue.
Advice: Consider increasing your iron intake through diet or supplements, and consult with a healthcare provider for appropriate testing.
When to See Doctor: If fatigue persists despite adequate sleep or is accompanied by symptoms like shortness of breath or dizziness.

metadata.advice: Consider increasing your iron intake through diet or supplements, and consult with a healthcare provider for appropriate testing.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue persists despite adequate sleep or is accompanied by symptoms like shortness of breath or dizziness.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Depression
Description: Depression can lead to overwhelming feelings of fatigue and exhaustion, even when adequate rest is obtained.
Advice: Seek professional help from a mental health provider to explore treatment options.
When to See Doctor: If feelings of sadness or hopelessness accompany fatigue, or if you have thoughts of self-harm.

metadata.advice: Seek professional help from a mental health provider to explore treatment options.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If feelings of sadness or hopelessness accompany fatigue, or if you have thoughts of self-harm.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Sleep Apnea
Description: Sleep apnea is a sleep disorder characterized by pauses in breathing during sleep, leading to disrupted sleep and persistent fatigue.
Advice: Consider undergoing a sleep study to diagnose the condition and discuss treatment options with a healthcare provider.
When to See Doctor: If you experience loud snoring, gasping for air during sleep, or excessive daytime sleepiness.

metadata.advice: Consider undergoing a sleep study to diagnose the condition and discuss treatment options with a healthcare provider.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience loud snoring, gasping for air during sleep, or excessive daytime sleepiness.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Fibromyalgia
Description: Fibromyalgia is a chronic condition that causes widespread pain and fatigue, often accompanied by sleep disturbances.
Advice: Engage in gentle exercise and consider cognitive behavioral therapy to manage symptoms.
When to See Doctor: If fatigue is accompanied by persistent pain or tenderness in multiple areas of the body.

metadata.advice: Engage in gentle exercise and consider cognitive behavioral therapy to manage symptoms.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If fatigue is accompanied by persistent pain or tenderness in multiple areas of the body.

text:
Symptom: fatigue
Possible Condition: Underactive Thyroid (Hypothyroidism)
Description: Hypothyroidism occurs when the thyroid gland does not produce enough hormones, leading to fatigue and other symptoms.
Advice: Get a thyroid function test and discuss potential treatment options with your healthcare provider.
When to See Doctor: If you experience weight gain, cold intolerance, or hair loss along with fatigue.

metadata.advice: Get a thyroid function test and discuss potential treatment options with your healthcare provider.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience weight gain, cold intolerance, or hair loss along with fatigue.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: gallbladder disease
Description: Gallbladder disease can cause nausea due to the disruption of bile flow, leading to digestive issues.
Advice: Avoid fatty foods and consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is accompanied by severe abdominal pain, fever, or jaundice.

metadata.advice: Avoid fatty foods and consult a healthcare provider for further evaluation.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is accompanied by severe abdominal pain, fever, or jaundice.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: food poisoning
Description: Food poisoning often results in nausea due to the ingestion of contaminated food or beverages.
Advice: Stay hydrated and rest; seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is severe, lasts more than 24 hours, or is accompanied by high fever or blood in vomit.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and rest; seek medical attention if symptoms persist.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is severe, lasts more than 24 hours, or is accompanied by high fever or blood in vomit.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: gastroenteritis
Description: Gastroenteritis, commonly known as stomach flu, can cause nausea due to inflammation of the stomach and intestines.
Advice: Maintain hydration and consider over-the-counter medications for symptom relief.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is accompanied by severe dehydration, persistent vomiting, or bloody stools.

metadata.advice: Maintain hydration and consider over-the-counter medications for symptom relief.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is accompanied by severe dehydration, persistent vomiting, or bloody stools.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: motion sickness
Description: Motion sickness occurs when the brain receives conflicting signals about motion, leading to nausea.
Advice: Try to focus on a stable point and consider over-the-counter motion sickness remedies.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms are frequent and disrupt daily activities.

metadata.advice: Try to focus on a stable point and consider over-the-counter motion sickness remedies.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms are frequent and disrupt daily activities.

text:
Symptom: nausea
Possible Condition: pregnancy
Description: Nausea during pregnancy, often referred to as morning sickness, affects many women in the early stages.
Advice: Eat small, frequent meals and consult your healthcare provider for safe remedies.
When to See Doctor: If nausea is severe or accompanied by weight loss or dehydration.

metadata.advice: Eat small, frequent meals and consult your healthcare provider for safe remedies.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is severe or accompanied by weight loss or dehydration.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: appendicitis
Description: Appendicitis is an inflammation of the appendix that can cause sudden, severe abdominal pain, typically starting around the navel and then moving to the lower right abdomen.
Advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect appendicitis.
When to See Doctor: If you experience sudden, severe abdominal pain, especially if it worsens over time.

metadata.advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect appendicitis.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience sudden, severe abdominal pain, especially if it worsens over time.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: Crohn's disease
Description: Crohn's disease is a type of inflammatory bowel disease that can cause chronic abdominal pain, often accompanied by diarrhea and weight loss.
Advice: Consult a healthcare provider for diagnosis and management options.
When to See Doctor: If you have persistent abdominal pain lasting more than three months or associated with other symptoms.

metadata.advice: Consult a healthcare provider for diagnosis and management options.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you have persistent abdominal pain lasting more than three months or associated with other symptoms.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: kidney stones
Description: Kidney stones can cause severe, colicky abdominal pain that comes in waves, often radiating to the back or groin.
Advice: Stay hydrated and seek medical help if the pain is severe or accompanied by blood in urine.
When to See Doctor: If you experience severe pain that comes and goes, especially with other symptoms like blood in urine.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and seek medical help if the pain is severe or accompanied by blood in urine.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe pain that comes and goes, especially with other symptoms like blood in urine.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: indigestion
Description: Indigestion can cause discomfort in the stomach area, often described as a feeling of fullness or burning.
Advice: Try over-the-counter antacids and avoid trigger foods; consult a doctor if symptoms persist.
When to See Doctor: If indigestion is frequent or accompanied by severe pain, vomiting, or weight loss.

metadata.advice: Try over-the-counter antacids and avoid trigger foods; consult a doctor if symptoms persist.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If indigestion is frequent or accompanied by severe pain, vomiting, or weight loss.

text:
Symptom: stomach pain
Possible Condition: constipation
Description: Constipation can lead to abdominal pain and discomfort due to the buildup of stool in the intestines.
Advice: Increase fiber intake and hydration; consider mild laxatives if necessary.
When to See Doctor: If constipation lasts more than a week or is accompanied by severe pain.

metadata.advice: Increase fiber intake and hydration; consider mild laxatives if necessary.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If constipation lasts more than a week or is accompanied by severe pain.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: Tonsillitis
Description: Tonsillitis causes inflammation and soreness of the tonsils, leading to a painful throat, especially when swallowing.
Advice: Stay hydrated and consider over-the-counter pain relievers to alleviate discomfort.
When to See Doctor: If you experience severe pain, difficulty breathing, or a high fever.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated and consider over-the-counter pain relievers to alleviate discomfort.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If you experience severe pain, difficulty breathing, or a high fever.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: Laryngitis
Description: Laryngitis is the inflammation of the larynx, which can cause a sore throat and hoarseness.
Advice: Rest your voice and drink warm fluids to soothe your throat.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or are accompanied by high fever.

metadata.advice: Rest your voice and drink warm fluids to soothe your throat.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist for more than a week or are accompanied by high fever.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: Epiglottitis
Description: Epiglottitis is a serious bacterial infection that causes swelling of the epiglottis, potentially blocking the airway.
Advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect epiglottitis.
When to See Doctor: Any signs of difficulty breathing or swallowing should prompt an urgent visit to a doctor.

metadata.advice: Seek immediate medical attention if you suspect epiglottitis.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: Any signs of difficulty breathing or swallowing should prompt an urgent visit to a doctor.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: Croup
Description: Croup is a viral infection that primarily affects children, causing inflammation of the larynx and trachea, leading to a sore throat.
Advice: Use a humidifier and keep your child calm to ease symptoms.
When to See Doctor: If your child has difficulty breathing or a persistent high fever.

metadata.advice: Use a humidifier and keep your child calm to ease symptoms.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If your child has difficulty breathing or a persistent high fever.

text:
Symptom: sore throat
Possible Condition: Common Cold
Description: A sore throat is often one of the first symptoms of a common cold, which is caused by viral infections.
Advice: Rest, stay hydrated, and consider over-the-counter remedies for relief.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms worsen or do not improve after a week.

metadata.advice: Rest, stay hydrated, and consider over-the-counter remedies for relief.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms worsen or do not improve after a week.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV)
Description: BPPV is a common inner ear disorder where tiny bits of calcium in the inner ear get loose, causing incorrect signals to the brain.
Advice: If you experience brief dizziness when tilting or turning your head, consider performing special head exercises or consult your doctor.
When to See Doctor: If symptoms persist or worsen, seek medical advice.

metadata.advice: If you experience brief dizziness when tilting or turning your head, consider performing special head exercises or consult your doctor.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If symptoms persist or worsen, seek medical advice.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: Cerebellar Stroke
Description: Cerebellar stroke occurs due to a blockage or rupture in the blood vessels of the cerebellum, leading to dizziness, headache, and potential loss of vision.
Advice: Seek emergency medical help immediately if you experience dizziness along with headache, slurred speech, or facial drooping.
When to See Doctor: Immediate medical attention is required for symptoms of stroke.

metadata.advice: Seek emergency medical help immediately if you experience dizziness along with headache, slurred speech, or facial drooping.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: Immediate medical attention is required for symptoms of stroke.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: Inner Ear Infection
Description: An inner ear infection can disrupt balance and cause dizziness, often accompanied by other symptoms like ear pain or hearing loss.
Advice: Consult a healthcare provider for appropriate diagnosis and treatment if you suspect an inner ear infection.
When to See Doctor: If dizziness is severe or accompanied by fever or hearing changes, see a doctor.

metadata.advice: Consult a healthcare provider for appropriate diagnosis and treatment if you suspect an inner ear infection.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness is severe or accompanied by fever or hearing changes, see a doctor.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: Dehydration
Description: Dehydration can lead to dizziness due to reduced blood volume and electrolyte imbalance, affecting overall body function.
Advice: Stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids, especially in hot weather or after exercise.
When to See Doctor: If dizziness persists despite hydration or is accompanied by severe symptoms, seek medical attention.

metadata.advice: Stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids, especially in hot weather or after exercise.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness persists despite hydration or is accompanied by severe symptoms, seek medical attention.

text:
Symptom: dizziness
Possible Condition: Low Blood Pressure
Description: Low blood pressure can cause dizziness, especially when standing up quickly, due to inadequate blood flow to the brain.
Advice: Rise slowly from sitting or lying positions and consider consulting a doctor if you frequently experience dizziness.
When to See Doctor: If dizziness occurs regularly or is accompanied by fainting, see a healthcare provider.

metadata.advice: Rise slowly from sitting or lying positions and consider consulting a doctor if you frequently experience dizziness.
metadata.when_to_see_doctor: If dizziness occurs regularly or is accompanied by fainting, see a healthcare provider.
